dict.string: Erasing a device with Disable Restore option is not possible. Please Enable Restore if you want to proceed.
Are you sure you want to erase all selected devices?
You are trying to update a device with "Disable Restore" option, are you sure you want to proceed

( SELF.restoreOptions.RequestInternalUseOnlyUnit == YES) OR (!( ( SELF.restoreOptions.SelectMediumSecurityBootPolicy == YES) OR ( SELF.restoreOptions.SelectPermissiveSecurityBootPolicy == YES) OR ( SELF.restoreOptions.DisableSIP == YES) OR ( SELF.restoreOptions.SetSIP1 == YES) OR ( SELF.restoreOptions.SetSIP2 == YES) OR ( SELF.restoreOptions.SetSIP3 == YES) OR ( SELF.restoreOptions.SetSMB2 == YES) OR ( SELF.restoreOptions.SetSMB3 == YES) ))
An Apple Silicon MacOS restore option which requires Internal Use Only Unit Entitlement was set, but such Entitlement was not requested. Are you sure you want to continue?
The Apple Silicon MacOS restore option DisableSIP was set, but it requires Permissive security mode which was not requested. Are you sure you want to continue?
You've set a custom ramdisk, however you didn't disable CS enforcement in your boot args which is typically a mistake. Are you sure you want to continue?
You are trying to restore a production device with lax boot args. Your device may panic on boot. Are you sure you want to continue?
Your restore boot args are missing rd=md0 which is typically a mistake. Are you sure you want to continue?
Your restore boot args are missing -restore which is typically a mistake. Are you sure you want to continue?
Your restore boot args are missing nand-enable-reformat=1 which is typically a mistake. Are you sure you want to continue?
Your restore boot args are missing common debug flags like "debug=0x14e" or "debug=0x144" which is typically a mistake. Are you sure you want to continue?
Your restore boot args are missing "serial=1" which is typically a mistake if you expect to get serial output during the restore. Are you sure you want to continue?
Your restore options disabled LwVM formatting which produces a non-resizable filesystem. Are you sure you want to continue?
You enabled baseband provisioning however Enable Factory Debug was not set. This will likely cause baseband provisioning to fail. Are you sure you want to continue?
Setting burnin-size in your restore bootargs during an update install is almost certainly a mistake and might destroy your filesystems. Are you sure you want to continue?

!( SELF.device.properties.ReleaseType != SELF.restoreOptions.ReleaseType && SELF.restoreOptions.CreateFilesystemPartitions == 0 && SELF.buildInfo.buildVersion != nil && SELF.buildInfo.buildVersion <= SELF.device.properties.BuildVersion && SELF.appPreferences.WillFailFIPS == YES )

This restore for device DEVICE_NAME will fail the FIPS integrity test. FIPS prevents users from update installing to a different release type with an equal or lesser build number relative to the currently installed build. To restore, your options are to: erase install, or update to a build newer than the one currently installed, or add the fips_mode=0 bootarg

(SELF.restoreOptions.RootToInstall == nil || SELF.restoreOptions.RootToInstall like "*.dmg" || SELF.restoreOptions.RootToInstall like "*.cpgz" || SELF.restoreOptions.RootToInstall like "*.cpio" || SELF.restoreOptions.RootToInstall like "*.cpio.gz" || SELF.restoreOptions.RootToInstall like "*.cpbz2" || SELF.restoreOptions.RootToInstall like "*.cpio.bz2" || SELF.restoreOptions.RootToInstall like "*.tgz" || SELF.restoreOptions.RootToInstall like "*.tar" || SELF.restoreOptions.RootToInstall like "*.tar.gz" || SELF.restoreOptions.RootToInstall like "*.tgz" || SELF.restoreOptions.RootToInstall like "*.tbz2" || SELF.restoreOptions.RootToInstall like "*.tar.bz2" || SELF.restoreOptions.RootToInstall like "*.zip" || SELF.restoreOptions.RootToInstall like "*/" ) && ( SELF.restoreOptions.OverlayRoot1 == nil || SELF.restoreOptions.OverlayRoot1 like "*.dmg" || SELF.restoreOptions.OverlayRoot1 like "*.cpgz" || SELF.restoreOptions.OverlayRoot1 like "*.cpio" || SELF.restoreOptions.OverlayRoot1 like "*.cpio.gz" || SELF.restoreOptions.OverlayRoot1 like "*.cpbz2" || SELF.restoreOptions.OverlayRoot1 like "*.cpio.bz2" || SELF.restoreOptions.OverlayRoot1 like "*.tgz" || SELF.restoreOptions.OverlayRoot1 like "*.tar" || SELF.restoreOptions.OverlayRoot1 like "*.tar.gz" || SELF.restoreOptions.OverlayRoot1 like "*.tbz2" || SELF.restoreOptions.OverlayRoot1 like "*.tar.bz2" || SELF.restoreOptions.OverlayRoot1 like "*.zip" || SELF.restoreOptions.OverlayRoot1 like "*/")

The root you are trying to install is an unsupported format. This root will cause your restore to fail. The root must be in a format supported by libarchive such as .cpio, .tar, .dmg or .zip, or an uncompressed directory structure.

((SELF.restoreOptions.OverlayRootsToInstall == nil) || (SELF.restoreOptions.OverlayRootsToInstall.@count == 0) || (SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.OverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.dmg").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.OverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.cpgz").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.OverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.cpio").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.OverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.cpio.gz").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.OverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.cpbz2").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.OverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.cpio.bz2").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.OverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.tar").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.OverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.tar.gz").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.OverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.tgz").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.OverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.tar.bz2").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.OverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.zip").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.OverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*/").@count == SELF.restoreOptions.OverlayRootsToInstall.@count ) )
Invalid root format in Overlay root list

The overlay root you are trying to install is an unsupported format. This root will cause your restore to fail. The overlay root must be in a format [.dmg, .cpgz, .cpio, .cpio.gz, .cpbz2, .cpio.bz2, .tar, .tar.gz, .tgz, .tbz2, .tar.bz2, .zip] or an uncompressed directory structure.

((SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSOverlayRootsToInstall == nil) || (SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSOverlayRootsToInstall.@count == 0) || (SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.dmg").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.cpgz").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.cpio").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.cpio.gz").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.cpbz2").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.cpio.bz2").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.tar").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.tar.gz").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.tgz").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.tbz2").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.tar.bz2").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.zip").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*/").@count == SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSOverlayRootsToInstall.@count ) )
Invalid root format in the Recovery OS Overlay root list

The recovery OS roots you are trying to install is an unsupported format. This root will cause your restore to fail. The recovery OS root must be in a format [.dmg, .cpgz, .cpio, .cpio.gz, .cpbz2, .cpio.bz2, .tar, .tar.gz, .tgz, .tbz2, .tar.bz2, .zip] or an uncompressed directory structure.

((SELF.restoreOptions.DataOnlyOverlayRootsToInstall== nil) || (SELF.restoreOptions.DataOnlyOverlayRootsToInstall.@count == 0) || (SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.DataOnlyOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.dmg").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.DataOnlyOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.cpgz").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.DataOnlyOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.cpio").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.DataOnlyOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.cpio.gz").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.DataOnlyOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.cpbz2").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.DataOnlyOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.cpio.bz2").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.DataOnlyOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.tar").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.DataOnlyOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.tar.gz").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.DataOnlyOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.tgz").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.DataOnlyOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.tbz2").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.DataOnlyOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.tar.bz2").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.DataOnlyOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.zip").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.DataOnlyOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*/").@count == SELF.restoreOptions.DataOnlyOverlayRootsToInstall.@count ) )
Invalid root format in data Overlay root list

The data only overlay roots you are trying to install is an unsupported format. This root will cause your restore to fail. The root must be in a format [.dmg, .cpgz, .cpio, .cpio.gz, .cpbz2, .cpio.bz2, .tar, .tar.gz, .tgz, .tbz2, .tar.bz2, .zip] or an uncompressed directory structure.

((SELF.restoreOptions.BaseSystemOverlayRootsToInstall== nil) || (SELF.restoreOptions.BaseSystemOverlayRootsToInstall.@count == 0) || (SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.BaseSystemOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.dmg").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.BaseSystemOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.cpgz").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.BaseSystemOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.cpio").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.BaseSystemOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.cpio.gz").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.BaseSystemOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.cpbz2").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.BaseSystemOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.cpio.bz2").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.BaseSystemOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.tar").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.BaseSystemOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.tar.gz").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.BaseSystemOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.tgz").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.BaseSystemOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.tbz2").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.BaseSystemOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.tar.bz2").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.BaseSystemOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.zip").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.BaseSystemOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*/").@count == SELF.restoreOptions.BaseSystemOverlayRootsToInstall.@count ) )
Invalid root format in basesystem Overlay root list

The basesystem overlay roots you are trying to install is an unsupported format. This root will cause your restore to fail. The root must be in a format [.dmg, .cpgz, .cpio, .cpio.gz, .cpbz2, .cpio.bz2, .tar, .tar.gz, .tgz, .tbz2, .tar.bz2, .zip] or an uncompressed directory structure.

((SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSBaseSystemOverlayRootsToInstall== nil) || (SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSBaseSystemOverlayRootsToInstall.@count == 0) || (SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSBaseSystemOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.dmg").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSBaseSystemOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.cpgz").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSBaseSystemOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.cpio").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSBaseSystemOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.cpio.gz").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSBaseSystemOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.cpbz2").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSBaseSystemOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.cpio.bz2").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSBaseSystemOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.tar").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSBaseSystemOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.tar.gz").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSBaseSystemOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.tgz").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSBaseSystemOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.tbz2").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSBaseSystemOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.tar.bz2").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSBaseSystemOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*.zip").@count + SUBQUERY(SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSBaseSystemOverlayRootsToInstall, $root, $root like "*/").@count == SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSBaseSystemOverlayRootsToInstall.@count ) )
Invalid root format in recovery basesystem Overlay root list

The recovery basesystemo overlay roots you are trying to install is an unsupported format. This root will cause your restore to fail. The root must be in a format [.dmg, .cpgz, .cpio, .cpio.gz, .cpbz2, .cpio.bz2, .tar, .tar.gz, .tgz, .tbz2, .tar.bz2, .zip] or an uncompressed directory structure.

The build you are trying to install is DOA for the device DEVICE_NAME. The restore will likely fail on this device. You can visit builds.swe.apple.com for more information. Are you sure you want to continue installing with this build?
We don't have livability information for the build you're about to install. You can visit builds.swe.apple.com for more information. Are you sure you want to continue?
Build is not livable for this device

The build you are trying to install is not livable for this device. The restore may succeed but you may not be able to boot. You can visit builds.swe.apple.com for more information. Are you sure you want to continue?
LwVM And APFS Both Enabled Warning
Your restore options have enabled both LwVM and APFS. This combination is not supported and LwVM option will be ignored. Are you sure you want to continue?
Updating Stockholm SE without Stockholm NFC Firmware
Your restore settings are configured to update the SE but not Stockholm NFC Firmware. This will likely fail. Are you sure you want to continue?
Enabled apfs and defined a system partition size
The system partition size option is ignored when Use APFS is set to true since space is shared on APFS volumes. Are you sure you want to continue?
You have chosen to restore a Desense install. It has a limited number of apps. Are you sure you want to continue?
PR3 detected a network reachability issue. Please confirm you're connected to the internal network and all your mounts are available before you click continue.

It looks like you don't have a kanzi/chimp cable plugged in, or we otherwise can't find or read from the device serial endpoint. Without serial logs, our ability to diagnose certain failures will be greatly limited. Do you want to continue?
It looks like the value selected for SEP EEPROM Test Delay is low, this will likely fail. Do you want to continue?
Wait for serial logs time is invalid
The wait time for serial logs on failure should be a value between 0 to 60 (seconds)

Could not pair with the device. The restore may fail because the available space cannot be verified. This could be caused by an untrusted host (to fix trust the host from the device). Are you sure you want to continue?
You are trying to perform an update install with an erase-install variant. Are you sure you want to continue?
You are trying to perform an Update install on a Mac which will only update iBoot and the RecoveryOS . Are you sure you want to continue?
Unable to retrieve the restore bundle using NFA
!( SELF.restoreOptions.CreateFilesystemPartitions == YES AND SELF.restoreOptions.InstallRecoveryOS == YES AND SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSOnly == YES )
Erase Restore with Recovery OS Only
You are perfomring erase restore with Recovery OS only option marked, this will result with device in recovery mode. Are you sure you want to continue?

( SELF.restoreOptions.OverlayRootsToInstall == nil AND SELF.restoreOptions.RootToInstall == nil AND SELF.restoreOptions.RecoveryOSOverlayRootsToInstall == nil AND SELF.restoreOptions.UpdateVolumeOverlayRootsToInstall == nil)
You are performing a restore with overlay roots selected. Are you sure you want to continue?

( SELF.restoreOptions.OverlayRootsToInstall != nil OR SELF.restoreOptions.RootToInstall != nil OR ( SELF.restoreOptions.RegenerateDyldSharedCache == nil AND SELF.restoreOptions.GenerateStaticTrustCache == nil ))
You are performing a restore with no roots selected but asking to regenerate a cache. Are you sure you want to continue?
You are trying to use restore behavior in non IPSW variant (the restore behavior will be ignored). Are you sure you want to continue?
DarwinOS is a minimal variant of our operating systems designed to support UI-less applications, and should not used unless intended.

!( SELF.restoreOptions.AuthInstallRestoreBehavior != nil AND SELF.restoreOptions.AuthInstallVariant CONTAINS "IPSW" AND ((SELF.restoreOptions.AuthInstallRestoreBehavior CONTAINS "Erase" AND !(SELF.restoreOptions.AuthInstallVariant CONTAINS "Erase")) OR (SELF.restoreOptions.AuthInstallRestoreBehavior CONTAINS "Update" AND !(SELF.restoreOptions.AuthInstallVariant CONTAINS "Upgrade"))) )
Restore Behavior Doesn't Match IPSW Variant
You are trying to use restore behavior that doesn't match the IPSW variant (the restore behavior will override the variant). Are you sure you want to continue?
You are specifying paths to roots that don't exist. Are you sure you want to continue?
It seem the tmp folder isn't writable which will prevent PR3 from doing the restore, please abort the restore and change /private/tmp folder permissions.
Update from Internal Build to IPSW warning
You are trying to update an internal build to IPSW. Are you sure you want to continue?
Notice the version you are updating to, seem to be a downgrade from the current installed version which might result in a failure, Are you sure you want to continue?
